krv.1TI_1_1: 우리 구주 하나님과 우리 소망이신 그리스도 예수의 명령을 따라 그리스도 예수의 사도 된 바울은
krv.1TI_1_2: 믿음 안에서 참 아들 된 디모데에게 편지하노니 하나님 아버지와 그리스도 예수 우리 주께로부터 은혜와 긍휼과 평강이 네게 있을지어다
krv.1TI_1_3: 내가 마게도냐로 갈 때에 너를 권하여 에베소에 머물라 한 것은 어떤 사람들을 명하여 다른 교훈을 가르치지 말며
krv.1TI_1_4: 신화와 끝없는 족보에 착념치 말게 하려 함이라 이런 것은 믿음 안에 있는 하나님의 경륜을 이룸보다 도리어 변론을 내는 것이라
krv.1TI_1_5: 경계의 목적은 청결한 마음과 선한 양심과 거짓이 없는 믿음으로 나는 사랑이거늘
krv.1TI_1_6: 사람들이 이에서 벗어나 헛된 말에 빠져
krv.1TI_1_7: 율법의 선생이 되려 하나 자기의 말하는 것이나 자기의 확증하는 것도 깨닫지 못하는도다
krv.1TI_1_8: 그러나 사람이 율법을 법있게 쓰면 율법은 선한 것인 줄 우리는 아노라
krv.1TI_1_9: 알 것은 이것이니 법은 옳은 사람을 위하여 세운 것이 아니요 오직 불법한 자와 복종치 아니하는 자며 경건치 아니한 자와 죄인이며 거룩하지 아니한 자와 망령된 자며 아비를 치는 자와 어미를 치는 자며 살인하는 자며
krv.1TI_1_10: 음행하는 자며 남색하는 자며 사람을 탈취하는 자며 거짓말하는 자며 거짓 맹세하는 자와 기타 바른 교훈을 거스리는 자를 위함이니
krv.1TI_1_11: 이 교훈은 내게 맡기신 바 복되신 하나님의 영광의 복음을 좇음이니라
krv.1TI_1_12: 나를 능하게 하신 그리스도 예수 우리 주께 내가 감사함은 나를 충성되이 여겨 내게 직분을 맡기심이니
krv.1TI_1_13: 내가 전에는 훼방자요 핍박자요 포행자이었으나 도리어 긍휼을 입은 것은 내가 믿지 아니할 때에 알지 못하고 행하였음이라
krv.1TI_1_14: 우리 주의 은혜가 그리스도 예수 안에 있는 믿음과 사랑과 함께 넘치도록 풍성하였도다
krv.1TI_1_15: 미쁘다 모든 사람이 받을 만한 이 말이여 그리스도 예수께서 죄인을 구원하시려고 세상에 임하셨다 하였도다 죄인 중에 내가 괴수니라
krv.1TI_1_16: 그러나 내가 긍휼을 입은 까닭은 예수 그리스도께서 내게 먼저 일절 오래 참으심을 보이사 후에 주를 믿어 영생 얻는 자들에게 본이 되게 하려 하심이니라
krv.1TI_1_17: 만세의 왕 곧 썩지 아니하고 보이지 아니하고 홀로 하나이신 하나님께 존귀와 영광이 세세토록 있을지이다 아멘
krv.1TI_1_18: 아들 디모데야 내가 네게 이 경계로써 명하노니 전에 너를 지도한 예언을 따라 그것으로 선한 싸움을 싸우며
krv.1TI_1_19: 믿음과 착한 양심을 가지라 어떤 이들이 이 양심을 버렸고 그 믿음에 관하여는 파선하였느니라
krv.1TI_1_20: 그 가운데 후메내오와 알렉산더가 있으니 내가 사단에게 내어준 것은 저희로 징계를 받아 훼방하지 말게 하려 함이니라
krv.1TI_2_1: 그러므로 내가 첫째로 권하노니 모든 사람을 위하여 간구와 기도와 도고와 감사를 하되
krv.1TI_2_2: 임금들과 높은 지위에 있는 모든 사람을 위하여 하라 이는 우리가 모든 경건과 단정한 중에 고요하고 평안한 생활을 하려 함이니라
krv.1TI_2_3: 이것이 우리 구주 하나님 앞에 선하고 받을실 만한 것이니
krv.1TI_2_4: 하나님은 모든 사람이 구원을 받으며 진리를 아는 데 이르기를 원하시느니라
krv.1TI_2_5: 하나님은 한 분이시요 또 하나님과 사람 사이에 중보도 한 분이시니 곧 사람이신 그리스도 예수라
krv.1TI_2_6: 그가 모든 사람을 위하여 자기를 속전으로 주셨으니 기약이 이르면 증거할 것이라
krv.1TI_2_7: 이를 위하여 내가 전파하는 자와 사도로 세움을 입은 것은 참말이요 거짓말이 아니니 믿음과 진리 안에서 내가 이방인의 스승이 되었노라
krv.1TI_2_8: 그러므로 각처에서 남자들이 분노와 다툼이 없이 거룩한 손을 들어 기도하기를 원하노라
krv.1TI_2_9: 또 이와 같이 여자들도 아담한 옷을 입으며 염치와 정절로 자기를 단장하고 땋은 머리와 금이나 진주나 값진 옷으로 하지 말고
krv.1TI_2_10: 오직 선행으로 하기를 원하라 이것이 하나님을 공경한다 하는 자들에게 마땅한 것이니라
krv.1TI_2_12: 여자의 가르치는 것과 남자를 주관하는 것을 허락지 아니하노니 오직 종용할지니라
krv.1TI_2_13: 이는 아담이 먼저 지음을 받고 이와가 그 후며
krv.1TI_2_14: 아담이 꾀임을 보지 아니하고 여자가 꾀임을 보아 죄에 빠졌음이니라
krv.1TI_2_15: 그러나 여자들이 만일 정절로써 믿음과 사랑과 거룩함에 거하면 그 해산함으로 구원을 얻으리라
krv.1TI_3_1: 미쁘다 이 말이여 사람이 감독의 직분을 얻으려 하면 선한 일을 사모한다 함이로다
krv.1TI_3_2: 그러므로 감독은 책망할 것이 없으며 한 아내의 남편이 되며 절제하며 근신하며 아담하며 나그네를 대접하며 가르치기를 잘하며
krv.1TI_3_3: 술을 즐기지 아니하며 구타하지 아니하며 오직 관용하며 다투지 아니하며 돈을 사랑치 아니하며
krv.1TI_3_4: 자기 집을 잘 다스려 자녀들로 모든 단정함으로 복종케 하는 자라야 할지며
krv.1TI_3_5: (사람이 자기 집을 다스릴 줄 알지 못하면 어찌 하나님의 교회를 돌아보리요)
krv.1TI_3_6: 새로 입교한 자도 말지니 교만하여져서 마귀를 정죄하는 그 정죄에 빠질까 함이요
krv.1TI_3_7: 또한 외인에게서도 선한 증거를 얻은 자라야 할지니 비방과 마귀의 올무에 빠질까 염려하라
krv.1TI_3_8: 이와 같이 집사들도 단정하고 일구이언을 하지 아니하고 술에 인박이지 아니하고 더러운 이를 탐하지 아니하고
krv.1TI_3_9: 깨끗한 양심에 믿음의 비밀을 가진 자라야 할지니
krv.1TI_3_10: 이에 이 사람들을 먼저 시험하여 보고 그 후에 책망할 것이 없으면 집사의 직분을 하게 할 것이요
krv.1TI_3_11: 여자들도 이와 같이 단정하고 참소하지 말며 절제하며 모든 일에 충성된 자라야 할지니라
krv.1TI_3_12: 집사들은 한 아내의 남편이 되어 자녀와 자기 집을 잘 다스리는 자일지니
krv.1TI_3_13: 집사의 직분을 잘한 자들은 아름다운 지위와 그리스도 예수 안에 있는 믿음에 큰 담력을 얻느니라
krv.1TI_3_14: 내가 속히 네게 가기를 바라나 이것을 네게 쓰는 것은
krv.1TI_3_15: 만일 내가 지체하면 너로 하나님의 집에서 어떻게 행하여야 할 것을 알게 하려 함이니 이 집은 살아계신 하나님의 교회요 진리의 기둥과 터이니라
krv.1TI_3_16: 크도다 경건의 비밀이여 그렇지 않다 하는 이 없도다 그는 육신으로 나타난 바 되시고 영으로 의롭다 하심을 입으시고 천사들에게 보이시고 만국에서 전파되시고 세상에서 믿은 바 되시고 영광 가운데서 올리우셨음이니라
krv.1TI_4_1: 그러나 성령이 밝히 말씀하시기를 후일에 어떤 사람들이 믿음에서 떠나 미혹케 하는 영과 귀신의 가르침을 좇으리라 하셨으니
krv.1TI_4_2: 자기 양심이 화인 맞아서 외식함으로 거짓말하는 자들이라
krv.1TI_4_3: 혼인을 금하고 식물을 폐하라 할 터이나 식물은 하나님이 지으신 바니 믿는 자들과 진리를 아는 자들이 감사함으로 받을 것이니라
krv.1TI_4_4: 하나님의 지으신 모든 것이 선하매 감사함으로 받으면 버릴 것이 없나니
krv.1TI_4_6: 네가 이것으로 형제를 깨우치면 그리스도 예수의 선한 일꾼이 되어 믿음의 말씀과 네가 좇는 선한 교훈으로 양육을 받으리라
krv.1TI_4_7: 망령되고 허탄한 신화를 버리고 오직 경건에 이르기를 연습하라
krv.1TI_4_8: 육체의 연습은 약간의 유익이 있으나 경건은 범사에 유익하니 금생과 내생에 약속이 있느니라
krv.1TI_4_9: 미쁘다 이 말이여 모든 사람들이 받을 만하도다
krv.1TI_4_10: 이를 위하여 우리가 수고하고 진력하는 것은 우리 소망을 살아계신 하나님께 둠이니 곧 모든 사람 특히 믿는 자들의 구주시라
krv.1TI_4_12: 누구든지 네 연소함을 업신여기지 못하게 하고 오직 말과 행실과 사랑과 믿음과 정절에 대하여 믿는 자에게 본이 되어
krv.1TI_4_13: 내가 이를 때까지 읽는 것과 권하는 것과 가르치는 것에 착념하라
krv.1TI_4_14: 네 속에 있는 은사 곧 장로의 회에서 안수 받을 때에 예언으로 말미암아 받은 것을 조심 없이 말며
krv.1TI_4_15: 이 모든 일에 전심전력하여 너의 진보를 모든 사람에게 나타나게 하라
krv.1TI_4_16: 네가 네 자신과 가르침을 삼가 이 일을 계속하라 이것을 행함으로 네 자신과 네게 듣는 자를 구원하리라
krv.1TI_5_1: 늙은이를 꾸짖지 말고 권하되 아비에게 하듯 하며 젊은이를 형제에게 하듯 하고
krv.1TI_5_2: 늙은 여자를 어미에게 하듯 하며 젊은 여자를 일절 깨끗함으로 자매에게 하듯 하라
krv.1TI_5_4: 만일 어떤 과부에게 자녀나 손자들이 있거든 저희로 먼저 자기 집에서 효를 행하여 부모에게 보답하기를 배우게 하라 이것이 하나님 앞에 받으실 만한 것이니라
krv.1TI_5_5: 참 과부로서 외로운 자는 하나님께 소망을 두어 주야로 항상 간구와 기도를 하거니와
krv.1TI_5_7: 네가 또한 이것을 명하여 그들로 책망 받을 것이 없게 하라
krv.1TI_5_8: 누구든지 자기 친족 특히 자기 가족을 돌아보지 아니하면 믿음을 배반한 자요 불신자보다 더 악한 자니라
krv.1TI_5_9: 과부로 명부에 올릴 자는 나이 육십이 덜 되지 아니하고 한 남편의 아내이었던 자로서
krv.1TI_5_10: 선한 행실의 증거가 있어 혹은 자녀를 양육하며 혹은 나그네를 대접하며 혹은 성도들의 발을 씻기며 혹은 환난 당한 자들을 구제하며 혹은 모든 선한 일을 좇은 자라야 할 것이요
krv.1TI_5_11: 젊은 과부는 거절하라 이는 정욕으로 그리스도를 배반할 때에 시집가고자 함이니
krv.1TI_5_13: 또 저희가 게으름을 익혀 집집에 돌아다니고 게으를 뿐 아니라 망령된 폄론을 하며 일을 만들며 마땅히 아니할 말을 하나니
krv.1TI_5_14: 그러므로 젊은이는 시집가서 아이를 낳고 집을 다스리고 대적에게 훼방할 기회를 조금도 주지 말기를 원하노라
krv.1TI_5_16: 만일 믿는 여자에게 과부 친척이 있거든 자기가 도와주고 교회로 짐지지 말게 하라 이는 참 과부를 도와주게 하려 함이니라
krv.1TI_5_17: 잘 다스리는 장로들을 배나 존경할 자로 알되 말씀과 가르침에 수고하는 이들을 더할 것이니라
krv.1TI_5_18: 성경에 일렀으되 곡식을 밟아 떠는 소의 입에 망을 씌우지 말라 하였고 또 일꾼이 그 삯을 받는 것이 마땅하다 하였느니라
krv.1TI_5_19: 장로에 대한 송사는 두세 증인이 없으면 받지 말 것이요
krv.1TI_5_20: 범죄한 자들을 모든 사람 앞에 꾸짖어 나머지 사람으로 두려워 하게 하라
krv.1TI_5_21: 하나님과 그리스도 예수와 택하심을 받은 천사들 앞에서 내가 엄히 명하노니 너는 편견이 없이 이것들을 지켜 아무 일도 편벽되이 하지 말며
krv.1TI_5_22: 아무에게나 경솔히 안수하지 말고 다른 사람의 죄에 간섭지 말고 네 자신을 지켜 정결케 하라
krv.1TI_5_23: 이제부터는 물만 마시지 말고 네 비위와 자주 나는 병을 인하여 포도주를 조금씩 쓰라
krv.1TI_5_24: 어떤 사람들의 죄는 밝히 드러나 먼저 심판에 나아가고 어떤 사람들의 죄는 그 뒤를 좇나니
krv.1TI_5_25: 이와 같이 선행도 밝히 드러나고 그렇지 아니한 것도 숨길 수 없느니라
krv.1TI_6_1: 무릇 멍에 아래 있는 종들은 자기 상전들을 범사에 마땅히 공경할 자로 알지니 이는 하나님의 이름과 교훈으로 훼방을 받지 않게 하려 함이라
krv.1TI_6_2: 믿는 상전이 있는 자들은 그 상전을 형제라고 경히 여기지 말고 더 잘 섬기게 하라 이는 유익을 받는 자들이 믿는 자요 사랑을 받는 자임이니라 너는 이것들을 가르치고 권하라
krv.1TI_6_3: 누구든지 다른 교훈을 하며 바른 말 곧 우리 주 예수 그리스도의 말씀과 경건에 관한 교훈에 착념치 아니하면
krv.1TI_6_4: 저는 교만하여 아무 것도 알지 못하고 변론과 언쟁을 좋아하는 자니 이로써 투기와 분쟁과 훼방과 악한 생각이 나며
krv.1TI_6_5: 마음이 부패하여지고 진리를 잃어버려 경건을 이익의 재료로 생각하는 자들의 다툼이 일어나느니라
krv.1TI_6_6: 그러나 지족하는 마음이 있으면 경건이 큰 이익이 되느니라
krv.1TI_6_7: 우리가 세상에 아무 것도 가지고 온 것이 없으매 또한 아무 것도 가지고 가지 못하리니
krv.1TI_6_8: 우리가 먹을 것과 입을 것이 있은즉 족한 줄로 알 것이니라
krv.1TI_6_9: 부하려 하는 자들은 시험과 올무와 여러 가지 어리석고 해로운 정욕에 떨어지나니 곧 사람으로 침륜과 멸망에 빠지게 하는 것이라
krv.1TI_6_10: 돈을 사랑함이 일만 악의 뿌리가 되나니 이것을 사모하는 자들이 미혹을 받아 믿음에서 떠나 많은 근심으로써 자기를 찔렀도다
krv.1TI_6_11: 오직 너 하나님의 사람아 이것들을 피하고 의와 경건과 믿음과 사랑과 인내와 온유를 좇으며
krv.1TI_6_12: 믿음의 선한 싸움을 싸우라 영생을 취하라 이를 위하여 네가 부르심을 입었고 많은 증인 앞에서 선한 증거를 증거하였도다
krv.1TI_6_13: 만물을 살게 하신 하나님 앞과 본디오 빌라도를 향하여 선한 증거로 증거하신 그리스도 예수 앞에서 내가 너를 명하노니
krv.1TI_6_14: 우리 주 예수 그리스도 나타나실 때까지 점도 없고 책망 받을 것도 없이 이 명령을 지키라
krv.1TI_6_15: 기약이 이르면 하나님이 그의 나타나심을 보이시리니 하나님은 복되시고 홀로 한 분이신 능하신 자이며 만왕의 왕이시며 만주의 주시요
krv.1TI_6_16: 오직 그에게만 죽지 아니함이 있고 가까이 가지 못할 빛에 거하시고 아무 사람도 보지 못하였고 또 볼 수 없는 자시니 그에게 존귀와 영원한 능력을 돌릴지어다 아멘
krv.1TI_6_17: 네가 이 세대에 부한 자들을 명하여 마음을 높이지 말고 정함이 없는 재물에 소망을 두지 말고 오직 우리에게 모든 것을 후히 주사 누리게 하시는 하나님께 두며
krv.1TI_6_18: 선한 일을 행하고 선한 사업에 부하고 나눠주기를 좋아하며 동정하는 자가 되게 하라
krv.1TI_6_19: 이것이 장래에 자기를 위하여 좋은 터를 쌓아 참된 생명을 취하는 것이니라
krv.1TI_6_20: 디모데야 네게 부탁한 것을 지키고 거짓되이 일컫는 지식의 망령되고 허한 말과 변론을 피하라
krv.1TI_6_21: 이것을 좇는 사람들이 있어 믿음에서 벗어났느니라 은혜가 너희와 함께 있을지어다

kjv.1TI_1_1: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the commandment of God our Saviour, and Lord Jesus Christ, which is our hope;
kjv.1TI_1_2: Unto Timothy, my own son in the faith: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.
kjv.1TI_1_3: As I besought thee to abide still at Ephesus, when I went into Macedonia, that thou mightest charge some that they teach no other doctrine,
kjv.1TI_1_4: Neither give heed to fables and endless genealogies, which minister questions, rather than godly edifying which is in faith: so do.
kjv.1TI_1_5: Now the end of the commandment is charity out of a pure heart, and of a good conscience, and of faith unfeigned:
kjv.1TI_1_6: From which some having swerved have turned aside unto vain jangling;
kjv.1TI_1_7: Desiring to be teachers of the law; understanding neither what they say, nor whereof they affirm.
kjv.1TI_1_8: But we know that the law is good, if a man use it lawfully;
kjv.1TI_1_9: Knowing this, that the law is not made for a righteous man, but for the lawless and disobedient, for the ungodly and for sinners, for unholy and profane, for murderers of fathers and murderers of mothers, for manslayers,
kjv.1TI_1_10: For whoremongers, for them that defile themselves with mankind, for menstealers, for liars, for perjured persons, and if there be any other thing that is contrary to sound doctrine;
kjv.1TI_1_11: According to the glorious gospel of the blessed God, which was committed to my trust.
kjv.1TI_1_12: And I thank Christ Jesus our Lord, who hath enabled me, for that he counted me faithful, putting me into the ministry;
kjv.1TI_1_13: Who was before a blasphemer, and a persecutor, and injurious: but I obtained mercy, because I did it ignorantly in unbelief.
kjv.1TI_1_14: And the grace of our Lord was exceeding abundant with faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.
kjv.1TI_1_15: This is a faithful saying, and worthy of all acceptation, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners; of whom I am chief.
kjv.1TI_1_16: Howbeit for this cause I obtained mercy, that in me first Jesus Christ might shew forth all longsuffering, for a pattern to them which should hereafter believe on him to life everlasting.
kjv.1TI_1_17: Now unto the King eternal, immortal, invisible, the only wise God, be honour and glory for ever and ever. Amen.
kjv.1TI_1_18: This charge I commit unto thee, son Timothy, according to the prophecies which went before on thee, that thou by them mightest war a good warfare;
kjv.1TI_1_19: Holding faith, and a good conscience; which some having put away concerning faith have made shipwreck:
kjv.1TI_1_20: Of whom is Hymenaeus and Alexander; whom I have delivered unto Satan, that they may learn not to blaspheme.
kjv.1TI_2_1: I exhort therefore, that, first of all, supplications, prayers, intercessions, and giving of thanks, be made for all men;
kjv.1TI_2_2: For kings, and for all that are in authority; that we may lead a quiet and peaceable life in all godliness and honesty.
kjv.1TI_2_3: For this is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Saviour;
kjv.1TI_2_4: Who will have all men to be saved, and to come unto the knowledge of the truth.
kjv.1TI_2_5: For there is one God, and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus;
kjv.1TI_2_6: Who gave himself a ransom for all, to be testified in due time.
kjv.1TI_2_7: Whereunto I am ordained a preacher, and an apostle, (I speak the truth in Christ, and lie not;) a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and verity.
kjv.1TI_2_8: I will therefore that men pray every where, lifting up holy hands, without wrath and doubting.
kjv.1TI_2_9: In like manner also, that women adorn themselves in modest apparel, with shamefacedness and sobriety; not with broided hair, or gold, or pearls, or costly array;
kjv.1TI_2_10: But (which becometh women professing godliness) with good works.
kjv.1TI_2_11: Let the woman learn in silence with all subjection.
kjv.1TI_2_12: But I suffer not a woman to teach, nor to usurp authority over the man, but to be in silence.
kjv.1TI_2_13: For Adam was first formed, then Eve.
kjv.1TI_2_14: And Adam was not deceived, but the woman being deceived was in the transgression.
kjv.1TI_2_15: Notwithstanding she shall be saved in childbearing, if they continue in faith and charity and holiness with sobriety.
kjv.1TI_3_1: This is a true saying, if a man desire the office of a bishop, he desireth a good work.
kjv.1TI_3_2: A bishop then must be blameless, the husband of one wife, vigilant, sober, of good behaviour, given to hospitality, apt to teach;
kjv.1TI_3_3: Not given to wine, no striker, not greedy of filthy lucre; but patient, not a brawler, not covetous;
kjv.1TI_3_4: One that ruleth well his own house, having his children in subjection with all gravity;
kjv.1TI_3_5: (For if a man know not how to rule his own house, how shall he take care of the church of God?)
kjv.1TI_3_6: Not a novice, lest being lifted up with pride he fall into the condemnation of the devil.
kjv.1TI_3_7: Moreover he must have a good report of them which are without; lest he fall into reproach and the snare of the devil.
kjv.1TI_3_8: Likewise must the deacons be grave, not doubletongued, not given to much wine, not greedy of filthy lucre;
kjv.1TI_3_9: Holding the mystery of the faith in a pure conscience.
kjv.1TI_3_10: And let these also first be proved; then let them use the office of a deacon, being found blameless.
kjv.1TI_3_11: Even so must their wives be grave, not slanderers, sober, faithful in all things.
kjv.1TI_3_12: Let the deacons be the husbands of one wife, ruling their children and their own houses well.
kjv.1TI_3_13: For they that have used the office of a deacon well purchase to themselves a good degree, and great boldness in the faith which is in Christ Jesus.
kjv.1TI_3_14: These things write I unto thee, hoping to come unto thee shortly:
kjv.1TI_3_15: But if I tarry long, that thou mayest know how thou oughtest to behave thyself in the house of God, which is the church of the living God, the pillar and ground of the truth.
kjv.1TI_3_16: And without controversy great is the mystery of godliness: God was manifest in the flesh, justified in the Spirit, seen of angels, preached unto the Gentiles, believed on in the world, received up into glory.
kjv.1TI_4_1: Now the Spirit speaketh expressly, that in the latter times some shall depart from the faith, giving heed to seducing spirits, and doctrines of devils;
kjv.1TI_4_2: Speaking lies in hypocrisy; having their conscience seared with a hot iron;
kjv.1TI_4_3: Forbidding to marry, and commanding to abstain from meats, which God hath created to be received with thanksgiving of them which believe and know the truth.
kjv.1TI_4_4: For every creature of God is good, and nothing to be refused, if it be received with thanksgiving:
kjv.1TI_4_5: For it is sanctified by the word of God and prayer.
kjv.1TI_4_6: If thou put the brethren in remembrance of these things, thou shalt be a good minister of Jesus Christ, nourished up in the words of faith and of good doctrine, whereunto thou hast attained.
kjv.1TI_4_7: But refuse profane and old wives' fables, and exercise thyself rather unto godliness.
kjv.1TI_4_8: For bodily exercise profiteth little: but godliness is profitable unto all things, having promise of the life that now is, and of that which is to come.
kjv.1TI_4_9: This is a faithful saying and worthy of all acceptation.
kjv.1TI_4_10: For therefore we both labour and suffer reproach, because we trust in the living God, who is the Saviour of all men, specially of those that believe.
kjv.1TI_4_12: Let no man despise thy youth; but be thou an example of the believers, in word, in conversation, in charity, in spirit, in faith, in purity.
kjv.1TI_4_13: Till I come, give attendance to reading, to exhortation, to doctrine.
kjv.1TI_4_14: Neglect not the gift that is in thee, which was given thee by prophecy, with the laying on of the hands of the presbytery.
kjv.1TI_4_15: Meditate upon these things; give thyself wholly to them; that thy profiting may appear to all.
kjv.1TI_4_16: Take heed unto thyself, and unto the doctrine; continue in them: for in doing this thou shalt both save thyself, and them that hear thee.
kjv.1TI_5_1: Rebuke not an elder, but intreat him as a father; and the younger men as brethren;
kjv.1TI_5_2: The elder women as mothers; the younger as sisters, with all purity.
kjv.1TI_5_3: Honour widows that are widows indeed.
kjv.1TI_5_4: But if any widow have children or nephews, let them learn first to shew piety at home, and to requite their parents: for that is good and acceptable before God.
kjv.1TI_5_5: Now she that is a widow indeed, and desolate, trusteth in God, and continueth in supplications and prayers night and day.
kjv.1TI_5_6: But she that liveth in pleasure is dead while she liveth.
kjv.1TI_5_7: And these things give in charge, that they may be blameless.
kjv.1TI_5_8: But if any provide not for his own, and specially for those of his own house, he hath denied the faith, and is worse than an infidel.
kjv.1TI_5_9: Let not a widow be taken into the number under threescore years old, having been the wife of one man.
kjv.1TI_5_10: Well reported of for good works; if she have brought up children, if she have lodged strangers, if she have washed the saints' feet, if she have relieved the afflicted, if she have diligently followed every good work.
kjv.1TI_5_11: But the younger widows refuse: for when they have begun to wax wanton against Christ, they will marry;
kjv.1TI_5_12: Having damnation, because they have cast off their first faith.
kjv.1TI_5_13: And withal they learn to be idle, wandering about from house to house; and not only idle, but tattlers also and busybodies, speaking things which they ought not.
kjv.1TI_5_14: I will therefore that the younger women marry, bear children, guide the house, give none occasion to the adversary to speak reproachfully.
kjv.1TI_5_15: For some are already turned aside after Satan.
kjv.1TI_5_16: If any man or woman that believeth have widows, let them relieve them, and let not the church be charged; that it may relieve them that are widows indeed.
kjv.1TI_5_17: Let the elders that rule well be counted worthy of double honour, especially they who labour in the word and doctrine.
kjv.1TI_5_18: For the scripture saith, thou shalt not muzzle the ox that treadeth out the corn. And, The labourer is worthy of his reward.
kjv.1TI_5_19: Against an elder receive not an accusation, but before two or three witnesses.
kjv.1TI_5_20: Them that sin rebuke before all, that others also may fear.
kjv.1TI_5_21: I charge thee before God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, and the elect angels, that thou observe these things without preferring one before another, doing nothing by partiality.
kjv.1TI_5_22: Lay hands suddenly on no man, neither be partaker of other men's sins: keep thyself pure.
kjv.1TI_5_23: Drink no longer water, but use a little wine for thy stomach's sake and thine often infirmities.
kjv.1TI_5_24: Some men's sins are open beforehand, going before to judgment; and some men they follow after.
kjv.1TI_5_25: Likewise also the good works of some are manifest beforehand; and they that are otherwise cannot be hid.
kjv.1TI_6_1: Let as many servants as are under the yoke count their own masters worthy of all honour, that the name of God and his doctrine be not blasphemed.
kjv.1TI_6_2: And they that have believing masters, let them not despise them, because they are brethren; but rather do them service, because they are faithful and beloved, partakers of the benefit. These things teach and exhort.
kjv.1TI_6_3: If any man teach otherwise, and consent not to wholesome words, even the words of our Lord Jesus Christ, and to the doctrine which is according to godliness;
kjv.1TI_6_4: He is proud, knowing nothing, but doting about questions and strifes of words, whereof cometh envy, strife, railings, evil surmisings,
kjv.1TI_6_5: Perverse disputings of men of corrupt minds, and destitute of the truth, supposing that gain is godliness: from such withdraw thyself.
kjv.1TI_6_6: But godliness with contentment is great gain.
kjv.1TI_6_7: For we brought nothing into this world, and it is certain we can carry nothing out.
kjv.1TI_6_8: And having food and raiment let us be therewith content.
kjv.1TI_6_9: But they that will be rich fall into temptation and a snare, and into many foolish and hurtful lusts, which drown men in destruction and perdition.
kjv.1TI_6_10: For the love of money is the root of all evil: which while some coveted after, they have erred from the faith, and pierced themselves through with many sorrows.
kjv.1TI_6_11: But thou, O man of God, flee these things; and follow after righteousness, godliness, faith, love, patience, meekness.
kjv.1TI_6_12: Fight the good fight of faith, lay hold on eternal life, whereunto thou art also called, and hast professed a good profession before many witnesses.
kjv.1TI_6_13: I give thee charge in the sight of God, who quickeneth all things, and before Christ Jesus, who before Pontius Pilate witnessed a good confession;
kjv.1TI_6_14: That thou keep this commandment without spot, unrebukable, until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ:
kjv.1TI_6_15: Which in his times he shall shew, who is the blessed and only Potentate, the King of kings, and Lord of lords;
kjv.1TI_6_16: Who only hath immortality, dwelling in the light which no man can approach unto; whom no man hath seen, nor can see: to whom be honour and power everlasting. Amen.
kjv.1TI_6_17: Charge them that are rich in this world, that they be not highminded, nor trust in uncertain riches, but in the living God, who giveth us richly all things to enjoy;
kjv.1TI_6_18: That they do good, that they be rich in good works, ready to distribute, willing to communicate;
kjv.1TI_6_19: Laying up in store for themselves a good foundation against the time to come, that they may lay hold on eternal life.
kjv.1TI_6_20: O Timothy, keep that which is committed to thy trust, avoiding profane and vain babblings, and oppositions of science falsely so called:
kjv.1TI_6_21: Which some professing have erred concerning the faith. Grace be with thee. Amen.

net.1TI_1_1: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the command of God our Savior and of Christ Jesus our hope,
net.1TI_1_2: to Timothy, my genuine child in the faith. Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord! Timothy’s Task in Ephesus
net.1TI_1_3: As I urged you when I was leaving for Macedonia, stay on in Ephesus to instruct certain people not to spread false teachings,
net.1TI_1_4: nor to occupy themselves with myths and interminable genealogies. Such things promote useless speculations rather than God’s redemptive plan that operates by faith.
net.1TI_1_5: But the aim of our instruction is love that comes from a pure heart, a good conscience, and a sincere faith.
net.1TI_1_6: Some have strayed from these and turned away to empty discussion.
net.1TI_1_7: They want to be teachers of the law, but they do not understand what they are saying or the things they insist on so confidently.
net.1TI_1_8: But we know that the law is good if someone uses it legitimately,
net.1TI_1_9: realizing that law is not intended for a righteous person, but for lawless and rebellious people, for the ungodly and sinners, for the unholy and profane, for those who kill their fathers or mothers, for murderers,
net.1TI_1_10: sexually immoral people, practicing homosexuals, kidnappers, liars, perjurers—in fact, for any who live contrary to sound teaching.
net.1TI_1_11: This accords with the glorious gospel of the blessed God that was entrusted to me.
net.1TI_1_12: I am grateful to the one who has strengthened me, Christ Jesus our Lord, because he considered me faithful in putting me into ministry,
net.1TI_1_13: even though I was formerly a blasphemer and a persecutor, and an arrogant man. But I was treated with mercy because I acted ignorantly in unbelief,
net.1TI_1_14: and our Lord’s grace was abundant, bringing faith and love in Christ Jesus.
net.1TI_1_15: This saying is trustworthy and deserves full acceptance: “Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners”—and I am the worst of them!
net.1TI_1_16: But here is why I was treated with mercy: so that in me as the worst, Christ Jesus could demonstrate his utmost patience, as an example for those who are going to believe in him for eternal life.
net.1TI_1_17: Now to the eternal king, immortal, invisible, the only God, be honor and glory forever and ever! Amen.
net.1TI_1_18: I put this charge before you, Timothy my child, in keeping with the prophecies once spoken about you, in order that with such encouragement you may fight the good fight.
net.1TI_1_19: To do this you must hold firmly to faith and a good conscience, which some have rejected and so have suffered shipwreck in regard to the faith.
net.1TI_1_20: Among these are Hymenaeus and Alexander, whom I handed over to Satan to be taught not to blaspheme.
net.1TI_2_1: First of all, then, I urge that requests, prayers, intercessions, and thanks be offered on behalf of all people,
net.1TI_2_2: even for kings and all who are in authority, that we may lead a peaceful and quiet life in all godliness and dignity.
net.1TI_2_3: Such prayer for all is good and welcomed before God our Savior,
net.1TI_2_4: since he wants all people to be saved and to come to a knowledge of the truth.
net.1TI_2_5: For there is one God and one intermediary between God and humanity, Christ Jesus, himself human,
net.1TI_2_6: who gave himself as a ransom for all, revealing God’s purpose at his appointed time.
net.1TI_2_7: For this I was appointed a preacher and apostle—I am telling the truth; I am not lying—and a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.
net.1TI_2_8: So I want the men in every place to pray, lifting up holy hands without anger or dispute. Conduct of Women
net.1TI_2_9: Likewise the women are to dress in suitable apparel, with modesty and self-control. Their adornment must not be with braided hair and gold or pearls or expensive clothing,
net.1TI_2_10: but with good deeds, as is proper for women who profess reverence for God.
net.1TI_2_11: A woman must learn quietly with all submissiveness.
net.1TI_2_12: But I do not allow a woman to teach or exercise authority over a man. She must remain quiet.
net.1TI_2_13: For Adam was formed first and then Eve.
net.1TI_2_14: And Adam was not deceived, but the woman, because she was fully deceived, fell into transgression.
net.1TI_2_15: But she will be delivered through childbearing, if she continues in faith and love and holiness with self-control.
net.1TI_3_1: This saying is trustworthy: “If someone aspires to the office of overseer, he desires a good work.”
net.1TI_3_2: The overseer then must be above reproach, the husband of one wife, temperate, self-controlled, respectable, hospitable, an able teacher,
net.1TI_3_3: not a drunkard, not violent, but gentle, not contentious, free from the love of money.
net.1TI_3_4: He must manage his own household well and keep his children in control without losing his dignity.
net.1TI_3_5: But if someone does not know how to manage his own household, how will he care for the church of God?
net.1TI_3_6: He must not be a recent convert or he may become arrogant and fall into the punishment that the devil will exact.
net.1TI_3_7: And he must be well thought of by those outside the faith, so that he may not fall into disgrace and be caught by the devil’s trap.
net.1TI_3_8: Deacons likewise must be dignified, not two-faced, not given to excessive drinking, not greedy for gain,
net.1TI_3_9: holding to the mystery of the faith with a clear conscience.
net.1TI_3_10: And these also must be tested first and then let them serve as deacons if they are found blameless.
net.1TI_3_11: Likewise also their wives must be dignified, not slanderous, temperate, faithful in every respect.
net.1TI_3_12: Deacons must be husbands of one wife and good managers of their children and their own households.
net.1TI_3_13: For those who have served well as deacons gain a good standing for themselves and great boldness in the faith that is in Christ Jesus. Conduct in God’s Church
net.1TI_3_14: I hope to come to you soon, but I am writing these instructions to you
net.1TI_3_15: in case I am delayed, to let you know how people ought to conduct themselves in the household of God, because it is the church of the living God, the support and bulwark of the truth.
net.1TI_3_16: And we all agree, our religion contains amazing revelation: He was revealed in the flesh, vindicated by the Spirit, seen by angels, proclaimed among Gentiles, believed on in the world, taken up in glory.
net.1TI_4_1: Now the Spirit explicitly says that in the later times some will desert the faith and occupy themselves with deceiving spirits and demonic teachings,
net.1TI_4_2: influenced by the hypocrisy of liars whose consciences are seared.
net.1TI_4_3: They will prohibit marriage and require abstinence from foods that God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and know the truth.
net.1TI_4_4: For every creation of God is good and no food is to be rejected if it is received with thanksgiving.
net.1TI_4_5: For it is sanctified by God’s word and by prayer.
net.1TI_4_6: By pointing out such things to the brothers and sisters, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus, having nourished yourself on the words of the faith and of the good teaching that you have followed.
net.1TI_4_7: But reject those myths fit only for the godless and gullible, and train yourself for godliness.
net.1TI_4_8: For “physical exercise has some value, but godliness is valuable in every way. It holds promise for the present life and for the life to come.”
net.1TI_4_9: This saying is trustworthy and deserves full acceptance.
net.1TI_4_10: In fact this is why we work hard and struggle, because we have set our hope on the living God, who is the Savior of all people, especially of believers.
net.1TI_4_12: Let no one look down on you because you are young, but set an example for the believers in your speech, conduct, love, faithfulness, and purity.
net.1TI_4_13: Until I come, give attention to the public reading of scripture, to exhortation, to teaching.
net.1TI_4_14: Do not neglect the spiritual gift you have, given to you and confirmed by prophetic words when the elders laid hands on you.
net.1TI_4_15: Take pains with these things; be absorbed in them, so that everyone will see your progress.
net.1TI_4_16: Be conscientious about how you live and what you teach. Persevere in this, because by doing so you will save both yourself and those who listen to you.
net.1TI_5_1: Do not address an older man harshly but appeal to him as a father. Speak to younger men as brothers,
net.1TI_5_2: older women as mothers, and younger women as sisters—with complete purity.
net.1TI_5_3: Honor widows who are truly in need.
net.1TI_5_4: But if a widow has children or grandchildren, they should first learn to fulfill their duty toward their own household and so repay their parents what is owed them. For this is what pleases God.
net.1TI_5_5: But the widow who is truly in need, and completely on her own, has set her hope on God and continues in her pleas and prayers night and day.
net.1TI_5_6: But the one who lives for pleasure is dead even while she lives.
net.1TI_5_7: Reinforce these commands, so that they will be beyond reproach.
net.1TI_5_8: But if someone does not provide for his own, especially his own family, he has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.
net.1TI_5_9: No widow should be put on the list unless she is at least sixty years old, was the wife of one husband,
net.1TI_5_10: and has a reputation for good works: as one who has raised children, practiced hospitality, washed the feet of the saints, helped those in distress—as one who has exhibited all kinds of good works.
net.1TI_5_11: But do not accept younger widows on the list, because their passions may lead them away from Christ and they will desire to marry,
net.1TI_5_12: and so incur judgment for breaking their former pledge.
net.1TI_5_13: And besides that, going around from house to house they learn to be lazy, and they are not only lazy, but also gossips and busybodies, talking about things they should not.
net.1TI_5_14: So I want younger women to marry, raise children, and manage a household, in order to give the adversary no opportunity to vilify us.
net.1TI_5_15: For some have already wandered away to follow Satan.
net.1TI_5_16: If a believing woman has widows in her family, let her help them. The church should not be burdened, so that it may help the widows who are truly in need.
net.1TI_5_17: Elders who provide effective leadership must be counted worthy of double honor, especially those who work hard in speaking and teaching.
net.1TI_5_18: For the scripture says, “Do not muzzle an ox while it is treading out the grain,” and, “The worker deserves his pay.”
net.1TI_5_19: Do not accept an accusation against an elder unless it can be confirmed by two or three witnesses.
net.1TI_5_20: Those guilty of sin must be rebuked before all, as a warning to the rest.
net.1TI_5_21: Before God and Christ Jesus and the elect angels, I solemnly charge you to carry out these commands without prejudice or favoritism of any kind.
net.1TI_5_22: Do not lay hands on anyone hastily and so identify with the sins of others. Keep yourself pure.
net.1TI_5_23: (Stop drinking just water, but use a little wine for your digestion and your frequent illnesses.)
net.1TI_5_24: The sins of some people are obvious, going before them into judgment, but for others, they show up later.
net.1TI_5_25: Similarly good works are also obvious, and the ones that are not cannot remain hidden.
net.1TI_6_1: Those who are under the yoke as slaves must regard their own masters as deserving of full respect. This will prevent the name of God and Christian teaching from being discredited.
net.1TI_6_2: But those who have believing masters must not show them less respect because they are brothers. Instead they are to serve all the more, because those who benefit from their service are believers and dearly loved. Summary of Timothy’s Duties Teach them and exhort them about these things.
net.1TI_6_3: If someone spreads false teachings and does not agree with sound words (that is, those of our Lord Jesus Christ) and with the teaching that accords with godliness,
net.1TI_6_4: he is conceited and understands nothing, but has an unhealthy interest in controversies and verbal disputes. This gives rise to envy, dissension, slanders, evil suspicions,
net.1TI_6_5: and constant bickering by people corrupted in their minds and deprived of the truth, who suppose that godliness is a way of making a profit.
net.1TI_6_6: Now godliness combined with contentment brings great profit.
net.1TI_6_7: For we have brought nothing into this world and so we cannot take a single thing out either.
net.1TI_6_8: But if we have food and shelter, we will be satisfied with that.
net.1TI_6_9: Those who long to be rich, however, stumble into temptation and a trap and many senseless and harmful desires that plunge people into ruin and destruction.
net.1TI_6_10: For the love of money is the root of all evils. Some people in reaching for it have strayed from the faith and stabbed themselves with many pains.
net.1TI_6_11: But you, as a person dedicated to God, keep away from all that. Instead pursue righteousness, godliness, faithfulness, love, endurance, and gentleness.
net.1TI_6_12: Compete well for the faith and lay hold of that eternal life you were called for and made your good confession for in the presence of many witnesses.
net.1TI_6_13: I charge you before God who gives life to all things and Christ Jesus who made his good confession before Pontius Pilate,
net.1TI_6_14: to obey this command without fault or failure until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ
net.1TI_6_15: —whose appearing the blessed and only Sovereign, the King of kings and Lord of lords, will reveal at the right time.
net.1TI_6_16: He alone possesses immortality and lives in unapproachable light, whom no human has ever seen or is able to see. To him be honor and eternal power! Amen.
net.1TI_6_17: Command those who are rich in this world’s goods not to be haughty or to set their hope on riches, which are uncertain, but on God who richly provides us with all things for our enjoyment.
net.1TI_6_18: Tell them to do good, to be rich in good deeds, to be generous givers, sharing with others.
net.1TI_6_19: In this way they will save up a treasure for themselves as a firm foundation for the future and so lay hold of what is truly life. Conclusion
net.1TI_6_20: O Timothy, protect what has been entrusted to you. Avoid the profane chatter and absurdities of so-called “knowledge.”
net.1TI_6_21: By professing it, some have strayed from the faith. Grace be with you all.
